license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.igxcbxn
title: Plaque Assay For Screening Viral Concentrates for Bacteriophage
author: Dr. Steven Wilhelm

steps_raw.instruction: Grow a 4 mL liquid culture of the bacterial isolate of interest in an appropriate growth medium overnight undetermined
Dilute the culture 10-20% and let it grow for ~4 hrs prior to plaque assay 04:00:00
Melt 0.6% top-agar in the microwave and let cool ~10-15 min 00:15:00
Pipet 1.5 mL liquid culture into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes (or enough liquid culture for 500 µL for each plate sample that you want to make).
Centrifuge 2 min at 14,000 rpm 00:02:00 Some bacterial isolates do not require centrifugation. This is something that needs to be empirically determined for each putative bacterial host.
Resuspend the remaining cells in 1.5 mL BG-11 media and mix well with pipet undetermined
Pipet 4 mL of molton top agar into sterile culture tubes undetermined
Keep top agar in a 45ºC head block to keep in a liquid state
In 500 µL of resuspended cells, add 100 µL of 0.22 µm filtered viral concentrate
Incubate for 15 min at an appropriate growth temperature for the bacterium in use 00:15:00
Pipet infected cells into molton top agar
Pour top agar onto the plate and spread
Let agar solidify at room temperature ~30-60 min 01:00:00
Flip plates over and place in an incubator at an appropriate growth temperature and light conditions for the bacterium in use
Check plates daily until a confluent lawn appears on plates. Plaques will be obvious clearings.
